item.title: BHP's incoming CEO met with Chinalco boss in China this week
item.description: BHP Group's incoming CEO, Brandon Craig, met with the вҖӢchairman of Chinalco in Beijing this вҖҢweek and discussed further cooperation, the Chinese aluminium giant said on Thursday.

item.title: Glencore charters supertanker to load oil from Middle East, sources say
item.description: Commodities trading firm Glencore has chartered a supertanker to load Middle Eastern crude for Asia, according to a shipping вҖӢsource and LSEG data, likely the first oil tanker to be вҖҢfixed for the route since the ceasefire in the U.S.-Iran war.

item.title: FedEx reaches tentative wage deal with pilots after years of talks
item.description: FedEx and a union representing more than 5,000 of its pilots вҖӢreached a tentative deal on Wednesday, paving вҖҢthe way for wages increases after years of negotiations.

item.title: Dollar steadies as fragile US-Iran ceasefire weighs on markets
item.description: The dollar caught its breath вҖӢin early Thursday trade after broad losses, as investors anxiously assessed whether a fragile two-week вҖҢceasefire between the United States and Iran would hold.

item.title: Asia stocks turn cautious as reality intrudes in Gulf
item.description: Asian share markets were in a more sober mood on вҖӢThursday as cracks quickly began to appear in the fragile Gulf ceasefire, nudging oil prices back up and reminding вҖҢinvestors the inflationary fallout will last for a long time yet.

item.title: Disney plans to cut 1,000 jobs, WSJ reports
item.description: Disney is planning to вҖӢcut as many вҖҢas 1,000 positions in the вҖӢcoming weeks, вҖӢmany of which вҒ will be вҖӢmade in the вҖӢcompany's marketing department, the Wall Street вҖӢJournal reported вҖӢon Wednesday, citing sources.

item.title: US crude futures rise after settling previous session with biggest fall in six years
item.description: U.S. crude futures rose on Thursday morning after settling вҖӢwith the biggest fall since вҖҢApril 2020 during the previous trading session, after U.S. President Donald Trump вҖӢagreed to a two-week вҖӢceasefire with Iran.

item.title: OpenAI will reserve portion of IPO shares for retail investors, CFO tells CNBC
item.description: OpenAI plans to reserve a portion of its shares from an initial public offering for individual investors, CFO Sarah Friar вҖӢtold CNBC on Wednesday, as the ChatGPT maker gears вҖҢup for a highly anticipated U.S. stock market listing.

item.title: US set to ease power limits on space-based broadband
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission is set to approve rule revisions this month to ease вҖӢpower limits on satellite spectrum use, giving a major boost to вҖҢspace-based broadband services such as SpaceX's Starlink.

item.title: US court declines to block Pentagon's Anthropic blacklisting for now
item.description: A Washington, D.C., federal appeals court on Wednesday declined to block the Pentagon's national security blacklisting of Anthropic for now, a win for вҖӢthe Trump administration that comes after another appeals court came to the opposite conclusion вҖҢin a separate legal challenge by Anthropic.

item.title: Constellation Brands posts smallerвҖ‘thanвҖ‘expected quarterly sales drop on steady Mexican beer demand
item.description: Corona beer maker Constellation Brands posted a smaller-than-expected drop in fourth-quarter sales on Wednesday, helped by stable demand for its Mexican lager вҖӢbrands such as Pacifico and Victoria.

item.title: Trading Day: Markets gorge on huge TACO
item.description: World markets soared on Wednesday and oil had its biggest fall in five years, as investors gave a hearty welcome to the ceasefire in the Iran war. The question now is whether this is just a mega-relief rally, or a major turning point in вҖӢsentiment.

item.title: FAA proposes to fine American Airlines for alleged drug, alcohol violations
item.description: The U.S. Federal Aviation Administration вҖӢsaid on Wednesday вҖҢit was proposing a $255,000 civil penalty against American вҖӢAirlines for allegedly вҖӢviolating drug and alcohol вҒ regulations.

item.title: FCC to vote on proposal to ban Chinese labs from testing US electronics
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission said on Wednesday it will vote this month on вҖӢa proposal to bar all Chinese labs from testing electronic devices such вҖҢas smartphones, cameras and computers for use in the U.S., widening a previous action targeting Beijing.

item.title: Southwest to fly wine for free in rare perk as airline fees rise
item.description: Southwest Airlines will fly wine for free from parts of the U.S. West Coast вҖӢafter launching new flights to California's wine вҖҢcountry, in a rare freebie as carriers pile on additional charges to cover high jet fuel costs.

item.title: Caterpillar elects Andrew Bonfield as new chief financial officer
item.description: Caterpillar вҖӢsaid on вҖҢWednesday it had appointed вҖӢAndrew вҖӢBonfield as вҒ its вҖӢchief вҖӢfinancial officer, effective вҖӢOctober вҖӢ1, 2026, вҖҢfollowing вҒ eight years with вҖӢthe вҖӢcompany.

item.title: Applied Digital beats quarterly revenue estimates on surging AI infrastructure demand
item.description: Applied Digital вҖӢon Wednesday beat estimates for quarterly вҖҢrevenue, driven by strong demand for its data center services from enterprises to support a boom вҖӢin generative AI applications.

item.title: Fed rate cut bets revived, a bit, by Iran war ceasefire
item.description: Federal Reserve policymakers may вҖӢconsider cutting interest rates later this year now that an agreement for a two-week ceasefire in the вҖҢIran conflict has eased concerns about a resurgence of inflation, but with uncertainty over the outlook for peace and oil prices still some 30% above their prewar levels, monetary policy easing is far from a done deal.

item.title: US banks could release $320 billion in capital with new draft rules, analysts say
item.description: Big U.S. banks may be able to release up to $320 billion in capital under revised draft rules unveiled by вҖӢregulators last month, Morgan Stanley analysts estimated on Wednesday.

item.title: US pump prices to stay elevated despite US-Iran ceasefire deal, market observers say
item.description: U.S. consumers will continue to pay вҖӢhigh prices to fill up their vehicles or purchase airplane tickets through the peak summer travel season, several market experts said on Wednesday, even as wholesale fuel prices вҖҢcooled after President Donald Trump announced a two-week ceasefire in the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Fed's Daly says US economic fundamentals in a 'good place'
item.description: San Francisco Federal Reserve President вҖҢMary Daly on Wednesday said the U.S. economy is fundamentally in a "good place" despite sharply higher oil prices from the Iran war and the uncertainty over how long the war will last.

item.title: Fed minutes show growing openness to rate hikes at March meeting
item.description: A growing group of Federal вҖӢReserve policymakers felt last month that interest rate hikes might be needed to counter inflation that continued to exceed вҖҢthe central bank's 2% target, particularly given the inflationary impact of the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, according to the minutes of their March 17-18 meeting.

item.title: Lufthansa cabin crew union calls one-day strike in Germany
item.description: Lufthansa faces a one-day strike this week, the airline's third labor disruption in two months.

item.title: Ex-Rio Tinto CEO's deep-sea mining firm to merge with Odyssey in $1 billion deal
item.description: A deep-sea mining firm led by former Rio Tinto CEO Tom Albanese will merge вҖӢwith Odyssey Marine Exploration in a $1 billion all-stock deal aimed at building one of the world's largest portfolios вҖҢof underwater mineral deposits.

item.title: Citigroup says AI helps speed account openings and systems upgrades
item.description: Citigroup is using artificial intelligence to speed up вҖҢaccount openings and the retirement of old software, the U.S. bank's head of technology, Tim Ryan, said, as the firm pushes to improve productivity.

item.title: Berkshire-owned PacifiCorp utility wins ruling that could reduce Oregon wildfire damages
item.description: An Oregon вҖӢappeals court on Wednesday ruled in favor of вҖҢPacifiCorp in litigation over a series of Oregon wildfires in 2020, saying a trial judge erred вҖӢin letting the case against the вҖӢBerkshire Hathaway-owned utility proceed as a class вҒ action.

item.title: Meta unveils first AI model from costly superintelligence team
item.description: Meta Platforms on Wednesday unveiled Muse Spark, the first artificial intelligence model from a costly team it assembled last year to catch вҖӢup with rivals in the AI race.

item.title: ItalyвҖҷs Serie A soccer league sounds out private equity for stake in overseas media unit, sources say
item.description: ItalyвҖҷs Serie вҖҢA soccer league has sounded out private equity investor interest for a minority stake in its international media rights business, three sources with knowledge of the matter told Reuters.

item.title: Exclusive: US SEC taps Gibson Dunn attorney to be new enforcement director, sources say
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission has tapped David Woodcock, вҖҢa Gibson Dunn lawyer and former agency official, to be its next enforcement director after the regulator's top cop abruptly quit last month, three sources familiar with the matter told Reuters.

item.title: Jeff Shell out as Paramount president, Deadline reports
item.description: Jeff вҖӢShell is out вҖҢas the president of Paramount Skydance , вҖӢDeadline reported on вҖӢWednesday.
